stories.name: Mstyslav Chernov
stories.story_summary: Oscar-winning, Pulitzer Prize-winning Ukrainian war journalist and AP photojournalist who deployed an Insta360 360-degree camera alongside traditional cameras on active Ukrainian battlefields. His documentary '2000 Meters to Andriivka' — co-produced with FRONTLINE/PBS and AP — used the Insta360 to capture frontline footage that could be reframed in post-production, a critical advantage when filming under live fire made controlling traditional framing impossible. The film won the Directing Award at Sundance 2025 World Cinema Documentary, received a BAFTA nomination, and won the George Polk Award, duPont-Columbia Award, and WGA Award. His previous film '20 Days in Mariupol' won the 2024 Academy Award for Best Documentary Feature.
stories.why_it_matters: A once-in-a-generation convergence of journalistic prestige and Insta360 technology: an Oscar and Pulitzer Prize winner using 360 cameras to document active combat, solving the real problem of framing under fire by capturing everything and reframing in post. The film is one of the most critically acclaimed documentaries of 2025. Insta360 has never featured or acknowledged Chernov.

stories.name: Nick and Caroline Robertson-Brown (Frogfish Photography)
stories.story_summary: Award-winning marine conservation photo-journalists and biologists (honours degrees in Environmental Biology and Animal Behaviour, four published books, Caribbean Tourism Organization Photo-journalists of the Year) who are creating 'OneOcean360: A Shark Story' — an immersive 360-degree film shot entirely on Insta360 X4 cameras in the Bahamas, designed to screen in planetariums, immersive domes, and schools across the UK. Ambassadors for Sharks4Kids and founders of SeaStraw, they integrate conservation education with every project. The film will ship with teacher educational packs.
stories.why_it_matters: Marine biologists and Sharks4Kids ambassadors deploying Insta360 X4 specifically for 360-degree dome and planetarium screenings — a use case that expands Insta360's reach into science education and conservation outreach. Entirely unrecognized by Insta360.

stories.name: Ben Forsyth
stories.story_summary: Australian documentary filmmaker (Think Films) directing 'Ultra,' a feature documentary about undefeated plant-based ultra-endurance cyclist Abdullah Zeinab preparing for the 4000 km Transcontinental Race across Europe. Forsyth uses Insta360 GO 3 cameras in a body-worn configuration to capture intimate footage of Zeinab's psychological inner experience during extreme endurance — a creative approach Think Films describes as 'a first in the global documentary film world.' Zeinab has won every race he has entered, including the 2019 6,800 km Trans-America Bike Race.
stories.why_it_matters: An entirely organic documentary project using Insta360's smallest camera to solve a real storytelling challenge: how to document the internal psychological landscape of an athlete during a 4000 km race. Think Films' innovation — body-worn Insta360 GO 3 for immersive psychological portraiture — is a genuine breakthrough in sports documentary filmmaking.

stories.name: Amalia Ulman
stories.story_summary: Argentinian-born artist and filmmaker who used Insta360 fish-eye views in 'Magic Farm,' her absurdist comedy that premiered in the Premieres section at Sundance Film Festival 2025 and was acquired by MUBI for global release. Ulman layered Insta360 fisheye shots with GoPros attached to neighborhood dogs and mixed-format visual grammar, creating a self-described 'rom-com shot as a skateboarding video.' The film stars Chloe Sevigny and Alex Wolff and received wide critical acclaim for its bold camera work.
stories.why_it_matters: Ulman deployed Insta360 as a narrative art cinema tool — not for adventure footage but for experimental storytelling in a prestige film (MUBI acquisition, Sundance Premieres). She expands Insta360's appeal to the fine-arts and international arthouse cinema community, a segment Insta360 has never targeted.

stories.name: Jenny Stock
stories.story_summary: Award-winning underwater cinematographer and BBC documentary producer named British Underwater Photographer of the Year 2024. Stock used the Insta360 X4 to win 3rd place in the Compact Camera category of the prestigious Underwater Photographer of the Year competition, and has integrated it into her marine conservation filming work alongside a 24-year broadcast TV career including National Geographic and BBC. A speaker at Go Diving Show 2025 and author of underwater photography books.
stories.why_it_matters: A credentialed broadcast filmmaker with National Geographic and BBC credits who has publicly championed Insta360 X4 as a competition-grade tool for underwater conservation filmmaking — winning an industry award with it. Her endorsement would resonate powerfully with professional filmmakers on the fence about adopting Insta360.
